name: router
description: Intelligent routing layer that analyzes requests and directs them to the most appropriate Skills, Agents, or Commands
capabilities: Analyzes user intent from natural language requests
Routes to optimal Skills, Agents, or Commands
Coordinates multi-step sequential and parallel workflows
Calculates confidence scores for routing decisions
Handles ambiguous requests with clarification prompts
Manages emergency fast-path routing for critical issues
author: AdamManuel-dev
securitySummary: All 213 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a pure documentation skill containing only YAML pattern definitions, markdown documentation, and TypeScript interface examples. The detected patterns are code examples within documentation files, not executable code. No file access, network calls, or command execution capabilities exist.
valueStatement: Complex development tasks require choosing the right tool from many options. The Router skill intelligently analyzes your request and routes it to the best available Skill, Agent, or Command with transparent explanations.
limitations: Does not execute code or modify files directly
Depends on available tools being installed
Routing accuracy depends on pattern matching rules

useCases.title: Learn the Ecosystem
useCases.description: New Claude Code users can describe what they want in natural language and get routed to the right tool automatically

useCases.title: Save Time on Tool Selection
useCases.description: Experienced developers skip the discovery phase with intelligent routing that matches requests to specialized tools

useCases.title: Coordinate Complex Workflows
useCases.description: Multi-step workflows like feature development, security audits, and refactoring are orchestrated automatically

promptTemplates.title: Fix Issues
promptTemplates.prompt: fix my typescript errors in the auth module

promptTemplates.title: Build Feature
promptTemplates.prompt: build a dashboard component with user metrics

promptTemplates.title: Review Code
promptTemplates.prompt: review my changes before I merge to main

promptTemplates.title: Emergency Fix
promptTemplates.prompt: URGENT production is down with payment processing errors

bestPractices: Describe what you want to accomplish, not the specific tool
Mention context like file paths or modules to improve routing accuracy
Use urgency keywords like URGENT or CRITICAL for emergency routing
antiPatterns: Requesting vague help without specifying the goal or domain
Expecting the router to execute code or modify files directly
Using the router when you already know the exact tool you need

faq.question: Does the router execute code or modify files?
faq.answer: No. The router analyzes requests and invokes other tools. It does not directly execute code or modify files.

faq.question: What happens if routing confidence is low?
faq.answer: The router asks clarifying questions to understand your intent better before making a routing decision.

faq.question: Can I use the router with custom tools?
faq.answer: The router routes to installed tools. Custom tools need to be properly registered to be routable.

faq.question: How does the router learn and improve?
faq.answer: The router tracks successful routes and user corrections to improve pattern matching over time.

faq.question: Does the router access my project files?
faq.answer: The router can gather context like git status and file types to inform routing decisions.

faq.question: How is this different from slash commands?
faq.answer: Slash commands require knowing the exact command. The router lets you describe what you want and finds the right tool.
